title: Sketch solver: constrained drags + DOF diagnosis
source: OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_sketch_solver.cpp:168-188 — testDofDiagnosisWithRedundantConstraint: 2 free points => DOF 4; +1 Horizontal on the line => DOF 3; + a REDUNDANT duplicate Horizontal => DOF still 3 (redundancy removes nothing); !isOverConstrained.
OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_sketch_solver.cpp:190-201 — testConflictingConstraintsDetected: two Fixed points + an incompatible HorizontalDistance(25) => isOverConstrained (genuine conflict).
OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_sketch_solver.cpp:33-52 — testVerticalConstraintDrag: line top(0,6)-bottom(0,0)+Vertical; drag bottom to (0,9); after: bottom.x==top.x==0, bottom.y==9.

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_sketch_solver.cpp:54-74 — testHorizontalConstraintDrag: line east(0,0)-west(12,0)+Horizontal; drag east to (7.25,0); after: east.y==west.y==0, east.x==7.25; dragResult.success.

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_sketch_solver.cpp:266-314 — rectangle drag: corners (0,0),(10,0),(10,6),(0,6); H on bottom+top, V on left+right; drag rp1 to (-2,-1); OPPOSITE corner rp3 stays at (10,6); rp2.y==rp1.y, rp4.x==rp1.x, rp3.x==rp2.x, rp3.y==rp4.y.

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_sketch_solver.cpp:205-242 — driven drag: p1(0,0)-p2(10,0), circle center(5,5) r2.5; Distance(p1,p2,10)+Radius(circle,2.5); solve success; drag p1 to (10,10) (consistent with the distance); p1 ends exactly (10,10).

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_sketch_solver.cpp:369-386 — large drag: line (0,0)-(200,0)+Horizontal, p2 Fixed; drag p1 to (500,300); p1.x==500 but p1.y==p2.y (stays on the horizontal => y~0, NOT 300).
OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_sketch_solver.cpp:316-358 — drag rollback determinism: after a failed solve within a gesture, endPointDrag() rolls back to the drag-start pose.

expected-values/proto_sketch_solver.txt — 'Sketch solver adapter prototype: OK', exit 0. NOTE: DOF and positions are asserted in the C++ source (not printed); stdout carries only qDebug solver traces (e.g. 'solve:start points=.. parameters=.. algorithm=1') + the terminal OK line proving the asserts held.

drags.provenance: proto_sketch_solver.cpp:309-314 (opposite corner fixed; adjacent corners track)

drags.setup: Distance(p1,p2,10)+Radius(circle,2.5); drag p1 to (10,10) consistent with the fixed distance
line (0,0)-(200,0) Horizontal, p2 Fixed; drag p1 to (500,300)

dragRollbackDeterminism.meaning: If a gesture has a failed solve after a successful move, EndGesture rolls back to the drag-start pose (not the last partial).
dragRollbackDeterminism.newStackMapping: SCHEMA §7.4 BeginGesture/SolveDrag(latest-wins)/EndGesture; final exact solve on pointer-up; Rust commits ONE undo command.

notes: DOF and drag positions are the load-bearing values, all from source asserts (the solver does not print them). Maps to the solver lane (SCHEMA §7.4): SketchUpsert returns {dof, state}; SolveDrag returns {status, dof, positions(changed-only)}. The redundant-vs-conflicting distinction (duplicate Horizontal = redundant, DOF unchanged; two Fixed + bad distance = conflicting) is a key correctness contract for the DOF badge. Uses PlaneGCS diagnose() (H4, TODO.md:148).
